title: AI Transformation Leader (AB-731)
subtitle: Lead AI Strategy, Governance, and Organisational Change
description: Define the business value of AI transformation and lead governance, ethics, and change management for enterprise AI adoption
overview: This course prepares you for the AB-731 certification exam. It is designed for leaders responsible for defining AI strategy, building operating models, ensuring ethical AI governance, and measuring business impact. You will learn to align AI initiatives with business objectives, build cross-functional AI adoption programmes, and manage the organisational change that comes with enterprise AI.

learningObjectives.title: AI Strategy and Business Value
learningObjectives.description: Define and communicate AI strategy that aligns with organisational goals and delivers measurable business outcomes

learningObjectives.title: AI Governance and Ethics
learningObjectives.description: Establish governance frameworks and ethical guidelines for responsible AI deployment

learningObjectives.title: AI Operating Models
learningObjectives.description: Design operating models that support scalable AI adoption across departments

learningObjectives.title: Change Management for AI
learningObjectives.description: Lead organisational change programmes that drive sustained AI adoption

modules.title: Defining AI Business Value
modules.description: Build a clear AI strategy tied to business objectives and KPIs
topics: Mapping AI opportunities to business challenges
Building an AI business case for executive leadership
Prioritising AI use cases by impact and feasibility

modules.title: AI Governance and Responsible AI
modules.description: Establish governance structures and ethical principles for AI
topics: Microsoft Responsible AI principles in practice
Data privacy and compliance in AI deployments
Risk assessment and mitigation for AI projects
Regulatory landscape and emerging AI legislation

modules.title: AI Operating Models
modules.description: Design scalable operating models for enterprise AI
topics: Centre of Excellence vs. federated AI models
Roles and responsibilities in an AI-ready organisation
Technology platform decisions for AI at scale

modules.title: Leading AI Change and Adoption
modules.description: Drive adoption through structured change management
topics: Change management frameworks for AI transformation
Building AI champions and internal advocacy

handsOnLabs.title: AI Strategy Workshop
handsOnLabs.description: Develop an AI strategy document for a realistic business scenario, including use-case prioritisation and success metrics

handsOnLabs.title: Governance Framework Design
handsOnLabs.description: Build an AI governance framework including policies, review processes, and risk assessments

handsOnLabs.title: Adoption Planning Exercise
handsOnLabs.description: Create an AI adoption roadmap with change management milestones and KPIs

prerequisites: Experience in a leadership or management role
Familiarity with Microsoft 365 and Copilot at a business level
No technical or programming experience required
targetAudience: IT directors and CIOs leading AI strategy
Digital transformation leads and programme managers
Business unit leaders adopting AI in their teams

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Microsoft Certified Trainer and cloud consultant who has helped multiple Dutch enterprises build their AI strategy and governance frameworks, from pilot to production rollout.

personalStory.story: I have seen AI projects fail not because the technology was wrong, but because nobody aligned the organisation around why it mattered. This course is built from the governance and strategy work I do with clients who are moving past the pilot stage and need a real operating model for AI.
